title: saxifragaceae
intro: The Saxifragaceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants. This family consists of approximately 470 species, which are commonly known as saxifrages, rockfoils, and tower cacti. Members of the Saxifragaceae family are typically found in a variety of habitats, including rocky areas, woodlands, and meadows, and exhibit a range of growth habits, from annuals to perennials. The Saxifragaceae family is native to the Northern Hemisphere, with species found in both the Old and New Worlds.
